danger_signals.visual_form: Red fluid discharge on hit/damage (green in Japanese version due to censorship)
danger_signals.meaning: Player or opponent taking damage; injury state indicator

danger_signals.visual_form: Character becomes visibly stunned/wobbling after round loss
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent weakened and vulnerable to finishing move execution

danger_signals.visual_form: Darkened/desaturated visual palette during fatality sequences (grayscale in JP version)
danger_signals.meaning: Critical danger state—match ending lethal strike imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Background spikes, acid pools, abysses visible in stage design
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threats; stage fatalities possible if opponent knocked into hazards

safety_signals.visual_form: Full or high health meter display above character
safety_signals.meaning: Character in safe/healthy state; capable of withstanding damage

safety_signals.visual_form: Consecutive win tally icons accumulating at screen edge
safety_signals.meaning: Player maintaining advantage; round progression toward match victory

safety_signals.visual_form: Babality or Friendship move execution animations
safety_signals.meaning: Alternative to fatality; opponent defeated but spared; mercy/comedic tone

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Raiden's thunder god protection; martial artist character archetypes
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Characters aligned with Outworld appear more alien/demonic in design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament setting transitions from neutral to Outworld realm visuals; stage aesthetics shift from Earth-like to interdimensional/alien environments

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Shao Kahn's rule; demonic/otherworldly character designs; Shang Tsung sorcerer archetype
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Outworld characters display more monstrous, non-human features compared to Earthrealm fighters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Outworld stages feature alien architecture, otherworldly lighting, supernatural elements in backgrounds (e.g., Pit II with mysterious figure/Blaze)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Raiden's summoning and gathering of warriors; character select screen presents mixed roster
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Characters enter Outworld territory as invaders; increased combat difficulty reflects 'away game' disadvantage

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Outworld dominance / Shao Kahn's home field advantage
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage environments become progressively more alien; final boss arena reflects Kahn's otherworldly power
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss characters (Shao Kahn, Kintaro) display enhanced difficulty; non-playable secret characters suggest shadowy forces at work

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Win tally icons accumulate; opponent roster depletes visually as defeated fighters are eliminated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Each match represents advancing deeper into Outworld; difficulty escalation toward final confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fatality sequence with desaturated/grayscale palette filter (color maintained in non-JP versions); stage-specific execution visuals (spikes, acid, abyss)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Finishing move animations signal irreversible match conclusion; narrative consequence of tournament combat

environmental_storytelling.example: The Pit II stage features mysterious flaming figure in background (later retconned as character Blaze), suggesting supernatural presences in Outworld; stage hazards (spikes, acid) communicate danger and exoticism of non-Earth location

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character roster composition as political narrative
environmental_storytelling.example: Mix of Earthrealm (Liu Kang, Raiden's chosen warriors) and Outworld representatives (Shang Tsung, Kintaro, Shao Kahn) visually establishes tournament conflict structure; newer characters like Kitana, Mileena, Kung Lao introduce political intrigue (dual loyalties, new factions within Outworld)

environmental_storytelling.technique: Visual palette shifts in fatality sequences
environmental_storytelling.example: Desaturation/grayscale during fatality animations (except JP version which uses color fade and censorship green for blood) communicates narrative weight of lethal finishers; visual distinction separates combat phase from execution phase
Shao Kahn and secret characters (Noob Saibot) lack finishing move capability, visually indicating their special status as ultimate threats/non-tournament participants; enforces narrative hierarchy

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard attack combos available; knockback moves push opponent across screen; mid-air special moves executable; faster gameplay pace reflects enhanced martial capability
Character enters daze animation; becomes immobilized; finishing move window opens (Fatality, Babality, Friendship, or Stage Fatality available)
Enhanced damage output; immunity to finishing moves; match represents critical progression gate; visual design emphasizes otherworldly power and dominance
Unavailable through standard progression; do not respond to finishing moves; presence communicates hidden layers in tournament structure and Outworld politics
Win icons accumulate at screen edge; character stance resets between rounds; consecutive victory tally reinforces momentum toward tournament completion

notes: SNES version is arcade-faithful but features regional censorship in Japanese release: blood recolored to green, fatality sequences desaturated to grayscale (except bridge stage fatality). This censorship communicates cultural sensitivity to graphic dismemberment while maintaining core mechanical communication of danger/defeat states. Faction communication relies heavily on character design archetypes (martial artist vs. demon/alien) and stage environment aesthetics rather than explicit color-coding or heraldry. The game communicates political hierarchy through boss immunity to finishing moves and secret character availability, suggesting hidden power structures within the tournament. Environmental storytelling emphasizes Outworld's 'otherness' through alien stage design, hazard placement, and mysterious background elements.
